ruleset.name: 2023.x Master Ruleset
ruleset.description: Master ruleset for 2023.x — all rules, all systems
ruleset.author: Itential

rules.name: Logging Level
rules.description: IAG4 Logging Level

messages.pass: Gateway logging level is set to INFO or WARNING, which is appropriate for production use
messages.fail: Gateway logging level is set too verbose (e.g., DEBUG or TRACE), which can generate excessive log volume and degrade performance in production

rules.name: HTTP Logging Level
rules.description: IAG4 HTTP Logging Level

messages.pass: HTTP logging level is set to INFO or WARNING, which is appropriate for production use
messages.fail: HTTP logging level is set too verbose, which can generate excessive log volume and degrade performance in production

rules.name: HTTP Server Threads
rules.description: IAG4 HTTP Server Threads

messages.pass: HTTP server threads are configured at 3x the CPU core count, which is the recommended for production workloads
messages.fail: HTTP server threads are below the recommended value of 3x the CPU core count — this can create a bottleneck under load and cause request timeouts

rules.name: Gateway4 Version
rules.description: Checks if Gateway4 version is compliant

messages.pass: Automation Gateway 4 is running a supported version (4.3.0 - 4.9.x)
messages.fail: Automation Gateway 4 version is outside the supported range — please upgrade to the latest supported release for security patches and stability improvements

rules.name: Gateway4 Audit Retention Days
rules.description: Audit Log Retention Days

messages.pass: Audit log retention has been changed from the default value, indicating it was intentionally configured
messages.fail: Audit log retention is still set to the default of 30 days — review whether this meets your organization's compliance and storage requirements

rules.name: Ansible Debug
rules.description: Check if Ansible Debug is Enabled

messages.pass: Ansible debug mode is disabled, which is the recommended setting for production
messages.fail: Ansible debug mode is enabled — this should be disabled in production as it increases log output and can expose sensitive data in job results

rules.description: Validate if LDAP Authentication is used

messages.pass: LDAP authentication is enabled for Gateway 4, providing centralized access control
messages.fail: LDAP authentication is not enabled for Gateway 4 — enabling LDAP is recommended in production for centralized user management and security

rules.name: Gateway4 LDAP Enabled

rules.description: Validates if --sync-config is enabled in service file

messages.pass: The --sync-config flag is enabled in the Gateway 4 service file, ensuring configuration changes are persisted
messages.fail: The --sync-config flag is not enabled — without this, configuration changes made through the Gateway UI may not survive a service restart

rules.name: Gateway4 Sync Config

rules.description: Validates the main database size

messages.pass: The Gateway 4 main database is within the recommended size limit of 1 GB
messages.fail: The Gateway 4 main database has exceeded 1 GB — large databases can slow down Gateway operations and should be reviewed for cleanup or archival

rules.name: Gateway4 Main Database Size

rules.description: Validates the audit database size

messages.pass: The Gateway 4 audit database is within the recommended size limit of 1 GB
messages.fail: The Gateway 4 audit database has exceeded 1 GB — consider adjusting the audit retention policy or archiving old audit records

rules.name: Gateway4 Audit Database Size

rules.description: Validates the exec history database size

messages.pass: The Gateway 4 execution history database is within the recommended size limit of 1 GB
messages.fail: The Gateway 4 execution history database has exceeded 1 GB — review the execution history retention settings to prevent performance degradation

rules.name: Gateway4 Exec History Database Size

rules.description: Validate if this property is enabled

messages.pass: A storage backend has been configured for Gateway 5
messages.fail: No storage backend is configured for Gateway 5 — the GATEWAY_STORE_BACKEND environment variable is not set

rules.name: Gateway Store Backend

rules.description: Validate TLS is enabled on gateway

messages.pass: TLS is enabled for Gateway 5 client connections, encrypting traffic between the gateway and external services
messages.fail: TLS is not enabled for Gateway 5 client connections — enabling TLS is strongly recommended to protect data in transit

rules.name: Gateway Client TLS

rules.description: Validate Gateway 5 logging level

messages.pass: Gateway 5 logging level is set appropriately for production use (INFO or higher)
messages.fail: Gateway 5 logging level is set to a verbose level (TRACE or DEBUG) — this generates excessive log output and should be raised to INFO or higher in production

rules.name: Gateway Logging Levels

rules.description: Validate if gateway manager is enabled

messages.pass: Gateway Connect (Gateway Manager integration) is enabled, allowing Platform to manage this gateway instance
messages.fail: Gateway Connect is not enabled — without this, Platform cannot manage or monitor this gateway instance remotely

rules.name: Gateway Connect Enabled

rules.description: Validate TLS Insecure set for Gateway

messages.pass: Insecure TLS mode is disabled for Gateway Connect, meaning certificate validation is enforced
messages.fail: Insecure TLS mode is enabled for Gateway Connect — this bypasses certificate validation and should be disabled in production for proper security

rules.name: Gateway Connect Insecure TLS

rules.description: Validate TLS is enabled on gateway

messages.pass: TLS is enabled for the Gateway 5 server, encrypting inbound connections
messages.fail: TLS is not enabled for the Gateway 5 server — enabling server TLS is recommended to encrypt all inbound traffic

rules.name: Gateway Server TLS

rules.description: Validate Enabled Gateway Features

messages.pass: The Ansible feature module is enabled on this Gateway 5 instance
messages.fail: The Ansible feature module is not enabled on this Gateway 5 instance

rules.name: Gateway Feature: Ansible

rules.description: Validate Enabled Gateway Features

messages.pass: The Host Keys feature module is enabled on this Gateway 5 instance
messages.fail: The Host Keys feature module is not enabled on this Gateway 5 instance

rules.name: Gateway Feature: Hostkeys

rules.description: Validate Enabled Gateway Features

messages.pass: The OpenTofu feature module is enabled on this Gateway 5 instance
messages.fail: The OpenTofu feature module is not enabled on this Gateway 5 instance

rules.name: Gateway Feature: OpenTofu

rules.description: Validate Enabled Gateway Features

messages.pass: The Python feature module is enabled on this Gateway 5 instance
messages.fail: The Python feature module is not enabled on this Gateway 5 instance

rules.name: Gateway Feature: Python

rules.description: Validate TLS is enabled on gateway

messages.pass: TLS is enabled for Gateway 5 runner connections, encrypting traffic to distributed execution runners
messages.fail: TLS is not enabled for Gateway 5 runner connections — enabling TLS is recommended to protect runner communication

rules.name: Gateway Runner TLS

rules.description: Validate Gateway Log Type

messages.pass: Console log output is using plain text format, which is standard for most deployments
messages.fail: Console log output is using JSON format — if this is intentional for log aggregation, ensure the file log format is also set consistently

rules.name: Gateway Console Log: JSON

rules.description: Validate Gateway Log Type

messages.pass: File log output is using plain text format, which is standard for most deployments
messages.fail: File log output is using JSON format — if this is intentional for log aggregation, ensure the console log format is also set consistently

rules.name: Gateway File Log: JSON

rules.description: Validate if redundancy is enabled to serve gateway manager

messages.pass: Gateway Connect high availability is not enabled — this gateway is running in standalone mode
messages.fail: Gateway Connect high availability is enabled — this gateway is part of an HA pair for Gateway Manager

rules.name: Gateway Connect Redundancy Check

rules.description: Validate Gateway Connect HA Primary

messages.pass: This Gateway Connect instance is configured as the HA primary node
messages.fail: This Gateway Connect instance is not configured as the HA primary — verify the HA pair assignment is correct

rules.name: Gateway Connect HA Primary Check

rules.description: Validate if Gateway Cerficate File is set

messages.pass: This Gateway Connect instance is configured as the HA primary node
messages.fail: This Gateway Connect instance is not configured as the HA primary — verify the HA pair assignment is correct

rules.name: Gateway Server Certificate File

rules.description: Validate if Gateway Cerficate File is set

messages.pass: A client TLS certificate file is configured for Gateway 5
messages.fail: No client TLS certificate file is configured — a certificate is required when client TLS is enabled (see IAG-013)

rules.name: Gateway Client Certificate File

rules.description: Validate if Gateway Cerficate File is set

messages.pass: A TLS certificate file is configured for Gateway Connect
messages.fail: No TLS certificate file is configured for Gateway Connect — a certificate is required when Connect TLS is enabled (see IAG-016)

rules.name: Gateway Connect Certificate File

rules.description: Validate if Gateway Cerficate File is set

messages.pass: A TLS certificate file is configured for the Gateway 5 runner
messages.fail: No TLS certificate file is configured for the Gateway 5 runner — a certificate is required when runner TLS is enabled (see IAG-022)

rules.name: Gateway Runner Certificate File

rules.description: Validate Gateway Version

messages.pass: Gateway 5 (iagctl) is running version 5.2 or newer
messages.fail: Gateway 5 (iagctl) is running a version older than 5.2 — please update to the latest release for security patches and new features

rules.name: Gateway Version Check

rules.description: Validate Gateway Custom Registries

messages.pass: At least one custom registry is configured in Gateway 5 (iagctl)
messages.fail: No custom registries are configured in Gateway 5 (iagctl) — custom registries are typically needed for organization-specific automation packages

rules.name: Gateway Custom Registries

rules.description: Validate Gateway Runner Anouncement Address

messages.pass: A runner announcement address is configured, allowing distributed runners to discover this gateway
messages.fail: No runner announcement address is configured — distributed runners will not be able to discover this gateway instance

rules.name: Gateway Runner Anouncement Address

rules.description: Validate Gateway Server Distributed Execution

messages.pass: Distributed execution is enabled, allowing workloads to be distributed across multiple runners
messages.fail: Distributed execution is not enabled — all automation workloads will run on the gateway server itself

rules.name: Gateway Server Distributed Execution
Mongo Version
rules.description: Verify MongoDB version is in supported list

messages.pass: MongoDB is running a supported version
messages.fail: MongoDB is running an outdated version that may contain unpatched security vulnerabilities — please upgrade to a supported release

rules.description: Validate Bind IP settings

messages.pass: MongoDB is bound to specific IP addresses, limiting network exposure
messages.fail: MongoDB is bound to 0.0.0.0 (all interfaces) — this exposes the database to all network traffic and is a security risk in production

rules.name: MongoDB Bind IP

rules.description: Read Concern for ReplicaSets

messages.pass: MongoDB read concern is set to 'majority', ensuring consistent reads across the replica set
messages.fail: MongoDB read concern is not set to 'majority' — this can lead to reading stale or uncommitted data from replica set members

rules.name: Mongo Repl defaultReadConcern

rules.description: Write Concern for ReplicaSets

messages.pass: MongoDB write concern is set to 'majority', ensuring writes are acknowledged by most replica set members before confirming
messages.fail: MongoDB write concern is not set to 'majority' — writes may be lost during a failover if they were only acknowledged by the primary

rules.name: Mongo Repl defaultWriteConcern

rules.description: Validates the total votes in the ReplicaSet

messages.pass: The replica set has 3 or more votes and the total is an odd number, which is required for proper election behavior
messages.fail: The replica set vote count is either below 3 or is an even number — this can cause election ties or split-brain scenarios during failover

rules.name: Replica Member Votes

rules.description: Validates if the Replica Set is Healthy

messages.pass: All members of the MongoDB replica set are reporting healthy status
messages.fail: One or more members of the MongoDB replica set are unhealthy — investigate replica member status immediately to prevent data availability issues

rules.name: Replica Set Healthy
Platform Default User
rules.description: Check if default user is enabled

messages.pass: The built-in default user account is disabled, which is the recommended security posture
messages.fail: The built-in default user account is still enabled — this account should be disabled in production and replaced with named user accounts for audit traceability

rules.name: Platform Core Logging Level
rules.description: Platform Pronghorn Core Logging Level

messages.pass: Platform core logging is set to INFO, which provides useful operational data without excessive volume
messages.fail: Platform core logging is set to a verbose level (e.g., DEBUG or TRACE) — this generates excessive log output and should be set to INFO for production

rules.name: Dead Process Check Enabled
rules.description: Check if the Dead Process Check is Enabled

messages.pass: Dead process checking is disabled, which avoids unnecessary overhead in most deployments
messages.fail: Dead process checking is enabled — this can cause elevated CPU usage from frequent process scanning and may impact platform performance

rules.name: Dead Process Check Interval
rules.description: Check the interval value for Dead Process Check

messages.pass: Dead process check interval is at least 60 seconds, which limits the performance impact of process scanning
messages.fail: Dead process check interval is below 60 seconds — frequent scanning can create unnecessary CPU overhead; increase this to at least 60 seconds

rules.name: Dead Process Check Max Period
rules.description: Check Max Period for Dead Process Check

messages.pass: Dead process check max period is at least 60 seconds, which is within the recommended range
messages.fail: Dead process check max period is below 60 seconds — this causes overly aggressive process monitoring and should be increased to reduce overhead

rules.name: Launch Timeout
rules.description: Platform Launch Timeout

messages.pass: Service launch timeout is at least 60 seconds, giving services adequate time to start up
messages.fail: Service launch timeout is below 60 seconds — services with slow dependencies may fail to start; increase this to at least 60 seconds

rules.name: Launch Delay
rules.description: Platform Launch Delay

messages.pass: Service launch delay is 5 seconds or less, which keeps startup times efficient
messages.fail: Service launch delay exceeds 5 seconds — this slows down platform startup and service restarts without providing meaningful benefit

rules.name: Server ID
rules.description: Check if Server ID is set

messages.pass: A unique server ID is configured, which is important for identifying this instance in HA deployments
messages.fail: No server ID is configured — setting a unique server ID helps distinguish this instance in multi-node or HA environments

rules.name: Broker Validation
rules.description: Check if Broker Validation is set

messages.pass: Broker validation is disabled, which is the recommended setting for production performance
messages.fail: Broker validation is enabled — this adds overhead to every message broker interaction and can impact platform performance in production

rules.name: Platform Version
rules.description: Check which version of Platform is used

messages.pass: Platform is running a current version within the supported 2023.1.x release range
messages.fail: Platform version is outside the expected 2023.1.x range — verify this is a supported release and consider upgrading if on an older version

rules.name: Node Version
rules.description: Check Node Version

messages.pass: Node.js version is within the supported range for IAP 2023.1 (v18.x)
messages.fail: Node.js version is outside the supported range for IAP 2023.1 — this version requires Node.js v18.x for compatibility and security

rules.name: Device Count Polling Interval
rules.description: Checks for Device Polling Interval

messages.pass: Device count polling interval is 24 hours or longer, which avoids unnecessary database queries
messages.fail: Device count polling interval is below 24 hours — frequent polling adds unnecessary load on the database; increase this to 24 or higher

rules.name: External Request Timeout
rules.description: Checks the timeout for external requests

messages.pass: External request timeout is set to at least 5 seconds, allowing adequate time for third-party API responses
messages.fail: External request timeout is below 5 seconds — very short timeouts can cause false failures when calling external services under load

rules.name: Log Max Files
rules.description: Check for the max log files kept

messages.pass: Maximum log file count is 5 or more, providing sufficient log history for troubleshooting
messages.fail: Maximum log file count is below 5 — this may not retain enough log history for troubleshooting; increase to at least 5

rules.name: Service Crash Recovery Max Retries
rules.description: Max Retries for Service Crash Recovery

messages.pass: Service crash recovery allows at least 5 retry attempts before giving up
messages.fail: Service crash recovery max retries not within range - changing this allows for more chances to recover from transient failures

rules.name: Log File Max Size
rules.description: Max Size for logs in platform

messages.pass: Individual log files can grow to at least 1 MB before rotation, which is sufficient for most environments
messages.fail: Maximum log file size is below 1 MB — very small log files rotate too frequently and can make troubleshooting difficult

rules.name: Auth Session TTL
rules.description: TTL Settings for Authentication

messages.pass: Authentication session TTL is at least 60 minutes, providing a reasonable session duration for users
messages.fail: Authentication session TTL is below 60 minutes — very short sessions force frequent re-authentication and can disrupt long-running workflows

rules.name: Webserver Cache Control Enabled
rules.description: Check if cache control is enabled

messages.pass: Webserver cache control is enabled, which improves page load performance for the Platform UI
messages.fail: Webserver cache control is disabled — enabling this improves UI responsiveness by allowing the browser to cache static assets

rules.name: Webserver HTTP Allowed Optional Verbs
rules.description: Check for Extra Verbs

messages.pass: No extra HTTP verbs have been added beyond the defaults, which is the expected secure configuration
messages.fail: Additional HTTP verbs have been configured — review these to ensure they are necessary and do not expand the attack surface

rules.name: Webserver HTTPS Enabled
rules.description: Check if HTTPS is Enabled in Platform

messages.pass: HTTPS is enabled for the Platform webserver, encrypting all browser-to-server traffic
messages.fail: HTTPS is not enabled for the Platform webserver — all traffic between users and Platform is unencrypted, which is a critical security issue in production

rules.name: Webserver HTTP Enabled
rules.description: Check if HTTP is Enabled in Platform

messages.pass: Unencrypted HTTP is disabled for the Platform webserver, preventing insecure connections
messages.fail: Unencrypted HTTP is still enabled — this should be disabled in production so that all connections are forced through HTTPS

rules.name: Webserver Timeout
rules.description: Platform Webserver Timeout

messages.pass: Webserver timeout is at least 5 minutes, which allows adequate time for long-running API operations
messages.fail: Webserver timeout is below 5 minutes — this can cause long-running operations (bulk imports, complex workflows) to fail prematurely

rules.name: Redis Connect Timeout
rules.description: Timeout for Redis Connections

messages.pass: Redis connection timeout is at least 30 seconds, which is sufficient to handle brief network interruptions
messages.fail: Redis connection timeout is below 30 seconds — increase this to at least 30 seconds to prevent unnecessary connection failures during brief network blips

rules.name: Mongo Auth Enabled
rules.description: Check to ensure mongo authenticaiton is enabled

messages.pass: MongoDB authentication is enabled, requiring credentials for database access
messages.fail: MongoDB authentication is not enabled — the database is accessible without credentials, which is a critical security risk in production

rules.name: Mongo Max Idle Time
rules.description: Check Max Idle Time for MongoDB

messages.pass: MongoDB idle connection timeout is configured, allowing stale connections to be recycled
messages.fail: MongoDB idle connection timeout is set to zero (disabled) — idle connections will never be reclaimed, which can exhaust the connection pool over time

rules.name: MongoDB Bypass Version Check
rules.description: Validate if bypass Check is enabled

messages.pass: MongoDB version check is not being bypassed, ensuring the database version is validated at startup
messages.fail: MongoDB version check is being bypassed — this usually means the database version is unsupported and may need to be upgraded

rules.name: Mongo URL
rules.description: Validate mongoURL for any additional properties

messages.pass: MongoDB connection URL includes query parameters for connection tuning (e.g., replica set name, timeouts)
messages.fail: MongoDB connection URL does not contain any query parameters — verify that required connection options like replica set and TLS settings are configured

rules.name: Mongo TLS Enabled
rules.description: Check if Mongo TLS is Enabled

messages.pass: TLS encryption is enabled for MongoDB connections, protecting database traffic in transit
messages.fail: TLS is not enabled for MongoDB connections — database traffic is unencrypted, which is a security risk in production

rules.name: Max Retries Per Request
rules.description: Validate max retries per request on Redis

messages.pass: Redis max retries per request is set to 20 or higher, providing adequate retry capacity during brief outages
messages.fail: Redis max retries per request is below 20 — increase this to handle transient connection issues without immediately failing requests

rules.name: Redis Max Heartbeat Write Retries
rules.description: Validate max hearbeat write retries

messages.pass: Redis heartbeat write retries is set to the recommended value of 20
messages.fail: Redis heartbeat write retries is not set to 20 — this value should be exactly 20 to ensure reliable heartbeat delivery during network instability

rules.name: Task Worker Enabled
rules.description: Validate if Task Worker is Enabled

messages.pass: Task workers are enabled, allowing background job processing to run in dedicated worker threads
messages.fail: Task workers are not enabled — without task workers, background jobs run on the main thread which can impact platform responsiveness

rules.name: Valut Read Only
rules.description: Validate if Vault is read only

messages.pass: Vault is configured as read-only from Platform, which is the recommended security posture
messages.fail: Vault is not set to read-only — Platform should only read secrets from Vault, not write to it; enable read-only mode for better security

rules.description: Validate if the legacy profile is enabled

messages.pass: No legacy profile is configured, which is the expected state for current deployments
messages.fail: A legacy profile is still configured — review whether this profile is still necessary or can be removed as part of a migration to the current configuration model

rules.name: Profile Enabled

rules.description: Validates the TLS Max Version

messages.pass: TLS maximum version is configured to the recommended method for this platform version
messages.fail: TLS maximum version is not configured to the expected value — update this to ensure the strongest supported encryption is available

rules.name: TLS Max Version

rules.description: Validates the TLS Min Version

messages.pass: TLS minimum version is 1.2 or higher, which blocks insecure legacy protocols (TLSv1.0, TLSv1.1)
messages.fail: TLS minimum version is below 1.2 — older TLS versions have known vulnerabilities and should be disabled in production

rules.name: TLS Min Version

rules.description: Validate if redis name contains any special chars

messages.pass: Redis database name contains only safe characters (alphanumeric, dots, hyphens, underscores)
messages.fail: Redis database name contains special characters or whitespace — this can cause connection issues or unexpected behavior

rules.name: Redis DB Name

rules.description: Validate if redis_db value is set

messages.pass: A Redis database index is configured
messages.fail: No Redis database index is configured — verify this is intentional

rules.name: Redis DB

rules.description: Validates the size of the AGManager pronghorn.json file

messages.pass: The AGManager pronghorn.json file is within the recommended maximum size of 20 MB
messages.fail: The AGManager pronghorn.json file exceeds 20 MB — an oversized configuration file can slow down AGManager startup and increase memory usage

rules.name: AGManager Pronghorn JSON Size

rules.description: Validates the frame_size value in NSO

messages.pass: NSO Netconf frame size is set to the recommended value of 16,376 bytes
messages.fail: NSO Netconf frame size is not set to 16,376 — an incorrect frame size can cause communication failures with NSO devices

rules.name: NSO Netconf Frame Size

rules.description: Validate if python version is supported

messages.pass: Python 3.9 is installed, which is required for IAP 2023.1
messages.fail: Python 3.9 is not installed — IAP 2023.1 requires Python 3.9 for core functionality

rules.name: IAP Python Version Check

rules.description: Validate if GatewayManager is latest

messages.pass: GatewayManager application is running version 1.7.23 or newer
messages.fail: GatewayManager application is running an older version than 1.7.23 — please update to the latest release for bug fixes and improved gateway management

rules.name: Gateway Manager Version Check

rules.description: Validate if service health threshold is too low

messages.pass: Service healthcheck unhealthy threshold is set to the recommended value of 3
messages.fail: Service healthcheck unhealthy threshold is not set to 3 — this controls how many consecutive failures mark a service as unhealthy

rules.name: Service Healthcheck Unhealthy Threshold

rules.description: Validate service health check interval

messages.pass: Service healthcheck interval is between 5 and 30 seconds, balancing responsiveness with overhead
messages.fail: Service healthcheck interval is outside the recommended range of 5-30 seconds — too low causes unnecessary overhead, too high delays failure detection

rules.name: Service Healthcheck Interval

rules.description: Validate service blacklist

messages.pass: No services are blacklisted — all installed services are allowed to run
messages.fail: One or more services are on the blacklist — review the blacklisted services to ensure they are intentionally excluded and not causing missing functionality

rules.name: Service Blacklist

rules.description: Validate if shutdownTimeout is greater than 0

messages.pass: Platform shutdown timeout is at least 3 seconds, allowing services to gracefully terminate
messages.fail: Platform shutdown timeout is below 3 seconds — services may not have enough time for graceful shutdown, which can cause data loss or corruption

rules.name: Service Shutdown Timeout

rules.description: Validate max pool size on mongo

messages.pass: MongoDB max connection pool size is 100 or higher, which is sufficient for production workloads
messages.fail: MongoDB max connection pool size is below 100 — this can cause connection bottlenecks under load; increase to at least 100

rules.name: Mongo Max Pool Size

rules.description: Validate if TLS is enabled for Redis

messages.pass: TLS is configured for Redis connections, encrypting traffic between Platform and Redis
messages.fail: TLS is not configured for Redis connections — database traffic is unencrypted, which is a security risk in production

rules.name: Redis TLS Enabled
Redis Configuration File
rules.description: Check if redis has a configuration file set

messages.pass: Redis has a configuration file path set, confirming it is running with a managed configuration
messages.fail: Redis does not have a configuration file set — it may be running with default settings, which are not suitable for production

rules.name: Redis MaxMemory Policy
rules.description: Check the maxmemory_policy in redis

messages.pass: Redis maxmemory-policy is set to 'noeviction', which prevents Redis from silently discarding data when memory is full
messages.fail: Redis maxmemory-policy is not set to 'noeviction' — with other policies, Redis may silently evict important data when memory pressure increases

rules.name: Redis Default User
rules.description: Check for the default user in redis

messages.pass: The 'default' user has been removed from Redis ACL, which prevents unauthenticated access
messages.fail: The 'default' user is still present in Redis ACL — this allows connections without explicit credentials and should be removed in production

rules.name: Redis Network Binding
rules.description: Verify binding is limited to required nodes

messages.pass: Redis is bound to a specific IP address, limiting which network interfaces accept connections
messages.fail: Redis is bound to 0.0.0.0 (all interfaces) — this exposes Redis to all network traffic and is a security risk in production

rules.name: Redis Version
rules.description: Verify Redis version is in supported list

messages.pass: Redis is running a supported version with current security patches
messages.fail: Redis is running an outdated version that may contain unpatched security vulnerabilities — please upgrade to a supported release

rules.name: TCP Keepalive
rules.description: Validate if TCP-keepalive is too low or too high

messages.pass: TCP keepalive is set between 60 and 120 seconds, which reliably detects dead connections without excessive overhead
messages.fail: TCP keepalive is outside the recommended range of 60-120 seconds — too low wastes bandwidth on health checks, too high delays detection of dead connections

rules.name: Redis Replica Check
rules.description: Checks if Redis has replica sets

messages.pass: Redis has one or more connected replicas, providing data redundancy and read scalability
messages.fail: Redis has no connected replicas — this means Redis is running in standalone mode without data redundancy

rules.name: Replica Ping Period
rules.description: Validate if replica period is too low or too high

messages.pass: Replica ping period is between 30 seconds and 3 minutes, which is within the recommended range
messages.fail: Replica ping period is outside the recommended range of 30-180 seconds — adjust this to balance replication health monitoring with network overhead

rules.name: Replica Timeout
rules.description: Validate if replica timeout is too low or too high

messages.pass: Replica timeout is between 30 seconds and 3 minutes, which allows adequate time for replica communication
messages.fail: Replica timeout is outside the recommended range of 30-180 seconds — too low causes false disconnections, too high delays failover detection

rules.name: Replica Backlog Size
rules.description: Validate if replica backlog size is too low

messages.pass: Replica backlog size is 512 MB or larger, providing sufficient buffer for temporary replication delays
messages.fail: Replica backlog size is below 512 MB — a small backlog may require full resynchronization after brief network interruptions instead of partial resync

rules.name: Replica Max Lag
rules.description: Validate if replica max lag time is too low or too high

messages.pass: Replica max lag is between 10 and 60 seconds, which is within the recommended range for production
messages.fail: Replica max lag is outside the recommended range of 10-60 seconds — this controls how far behind a replica can fall before it is considered unhealthy

rules.name: No appendfsync on rewrite
rules.description: Validate if enabled

messages.pass: The no-appendfsync-on-rewrite setting is enabled, which prevents disk I/O bottlenecks during background rewrites
messages.fail: The no-appendfsync-on-rewrite setting is not enabled — enabling this improves write performance during background save operations by deferring fsync calls

rules.name: Client Output Buffer Limit Replica
rules.description: Validate buffer limit for replicas

messages.pass: Client output buffer limits for replicas are set to the recommended values (512mb hard, 128mb soft, 60s window)
messages.fail: Client output buffer limits for replicas do not match the recommended configuration — incorrect limits can cause replica disconnections during heavy write loads

rules.name: Sentinel Down After Milliseconds
rules.description: Validate how long sentinel should take to see if master is down

messages.pass: Sentinel down-after-milliseconds is set to 5 seconds (5000ms), which is the recommended detection threshold
messages.fail: Sentinel down-after-milliseconds is not set to 5000ms — this controls how quickly Sentinel detects a master failure; the recommended value is 5 seconds

rules.name: Sentinel Parallel Syncs
rules.description: validate if this value is set to 1 for parallel syncs

messages.pass: Sentinel parallel-syncs is set to 1, ensuring only one replica syncs at a time during failover to minimize performance impact
messages.fail: Sentinel parallel-syncs is not set to 1 — allowing multiple replicas to sync simultaneously during failover can overload the new master

rules.name: Sentinel Failover Timeout
rules.description: validate if failover timeout is not too low or too high

messages.pass: Sentinel failover timeout is set to 60 seconds (60000ms), which is the recommended value for production
messages.fail: Sentinel failover timeout is not set to 60000ms — this controls the maximum time Sentinel waits for a failover to complete; the recommended value is 60 seconds
